purpose: Workstream C fixtures are a two-sided proof: the fixture must be demonstrably RED against pre-fix main, then flipped to enforced-green by the fix bead. This file records the red half so the flip is auditable rather than asserted.

expected_failure.flip_action: delete the #[ignore] attribute; no assertion change is permitted

red_run.result: FAILED. 0 passed; 1 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 5 filtered out
red_run.panic_message: assertion `left != right` failed: 'wrong typ (plain "JWT")' and 'missing client_id' are different failures and must produce different diagnostics; both said: Bearer resource_metadata="https://oraclemcp.c1-fixture.example/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource", error="invalid_token"
red_run.root_cause: token_error_code (crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/mod.rs:517-523) collapses Malformed / BadSignature / AudienceMismatch / UntrustedIssuer / Expired into one invalid_token code, and no error_description is emitted, so every rejected bearer reads identically to the client.

green_half.result: ok. 5 passed; 0 failed; 1 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out
green_half.note: The acceptance half passes against pre-fix main. Plan sec.4-C1 predicted that asymmetry: the verifier was never broken (sec.A.5.2 refutes P1-11 as a code defect); what was missing was proof that an externally minted token is accepted at all, plus a usable diagnostic when one is not.

external_mint.note: Token and secret constants were minted once out of tree and committed as opaque strings. Regenerating them from repository code would restore exactly the self-reference this fixture exists to remove.
